chunks.page_summary: This page details the mathematical model and coefficients required for API density measurement using Rheonics' SRD inline density meter, emphasizing temperature and pressure corrections for hydrocarbon groups.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
Degree API density measurement with online process density meter SRD
Modified on: Mon, 20 Nov, 2023 at 3:26 PM

What products are involved in this article?
This article is based on the use of RCP (Rheonics Control Panel (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000297177-running-the-rcp-program)) connected to the SME (Smart Measurement Electronics).

What is the purpose of this article?
Explain the process to get temperature and pressure corrections specific to the hydrocarbon group, using API density referral converted into API Degrees. Usual applications are w

1. Math model

Shown below is the mathematical formula for API Specific Gravity

ρ_comp = ρ_L * e^{α_ref * ΔT * [1+0.8*α_ref * ΔT]}
α_ref = (K_0 + K_1 * ρ_ref) / ρ_ref^2
SG_ref temp = ρ_comp / ρ_water
ΔT = T_L - T_ref

Equation 1. API Specific Gravity

APIdg = 141.5 / SG60 - 131.5

Equation 2. API Degrees calculation

The SRD (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/) sensor can measure inline density, viscosity, and temperature, in addition, we can use multiple mathematical models to calculate the concentration of a mix. To calculate the API degrees of a fluid we can use Equation 1, where:

SGref temp: Specific gravity at the reference temperature.
pcomp: Compensated density.
pref: Reference density, is the density around which the sensor will compensate the readings. This is defined and should be specified by the user.
pL: Inline Density measured.
pwater: Water density.
K0 and K1: Coefficients, specified by the user. These coefficients can be found in the API-ASTM tables.
TL: Inline temperature measured in °C
Tref: Reference temperature, is the temperature around which the sensor will compensate the readings. This is defined and should be specified by the user.
APIdg: Api degrees.

2. Checking the data

All density, viscosity compensation, and concentration models can be loaded into the sensor through the RCP software from Rheonics (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000297177-running-the-rcp-program), in the "Calculation" tab. The API degrees model needs K1 and K0 coefficients, those values can be found in the API-ASTM-IP petroleum measurement tables, as shown below in Table 1. Also, it needs the water density in Kg/m3 at a reference temperature.

Table 1. API-ASTM-IP petroleum measurement table

Temperature [°C] | Density [g/cc] | K0 | K1
Crude Oil | 771 - 981 | 613.97226 | 0.00000
Gasolines | 654 - 779 | 346.42278 | 0.43884
Kerosines | 779 - 839 | 594.54180 | 0.00000
Fuel Oils | 839 - 1075 | 186.96960 | 0.48618

3. How to load models into the Sensor electronics?
key_facts: The SRD sensor can measure inline density, viscosity, and temperature.
Multiple mathematical models can be used to calculate the concentration of a mix.
API degrees of a fluid can be calculated using Equation 1 (API Specific Gravity) and Equation 2 (API Degrees calculation).
K0 and K1 coefficients, along with water density, are needed for the API degrees model.
K0 and K1 coefficients are specified by the user and can be found in API-ASTM tables.
Density, viscosity compensation, and concentration models can be loaded into the sensor through the RCP software from Rheonics, in the 'Calculation' tab.

chunks.page_summary: This page describes how to configure the Rheonics Control Panel for calculating API degrees and specific gravity, including loading models and handling sensor outputs.
chunks.extracted_text:
Rheonics Control Panel 5.4.0.6
Measurement | Events | Settings | Service | Diagnostics | Communication | Temp. Control | Scaling | Calculation | Logger

Advanced Calculations | Thermal Compensation of Density, Viscosity

Select Measurement to Apply Model: Concentration
Calculated Value: 0.971613
Status: [Green Status Indicator]
Select Model from List: API Degrees

ASTM-API Model
Specific Gravity

Options: Crude Oil; 771 - 981 Kg/m3
K0: 613.97226
K1: 0
Tref: 60 °F
Water Ref. Density [Kg/m3]: 999.07
Units: °API

APIdg = 141.5 / SG60 - 131.5

UPLOAD MODEL

Change SMET Output
LCD Display (for TRD Only) Select Line to Display: Line 1
Load Configuration
Output on 4-20 mA channel Select channel number: Ch2
Access measurement with applied model over Modbus RTU/TCP register: 208
Calculation History

Figure 1. Calculation tab for API degrees

In the following steps, we will show how to load the model into the sensor electronics:
1. In the "Select Measurement to Apply Model" dropdown list select "Concentration".
2. In the "Select Model from the list" dropdown list select "API Degrees". When you select the model, you will get more more information about the specific equation used and the coefficients you will need.
3. The "Options" list shows a list of the most common K0 and K1 values for the hydrocarbon group. Also, you can choose the "Custom" option and input your own coefficients K0 and K1.
4. Input "K0" coefficient. For this example, it has a value of 613.97.
5. Input "K1" coefficient. For this example, it has a value of 0,0000.
6. Input "Reference temperature" for the water density, in this example, is 60 °F.
7. Input "Water density" in Kg/m3 at the reference temperature, in this example, is 999.07 Kg/m3.
8. Click "Upload Model" to load the model settings into sensor electronics. The button should turn GREEN for a couple of seconds if the action was successful or turn RED if the operation was unsuccessful. This action will refresh the "Calculated Value".

Figure 2. Specific Gravity calculated

9. Click "Load Configuration" to update the display and channel configurations in sensor electronics.
10. The "Calculation History" button allows you to open a file with the last uploaded models.

3.1. Handling Sensor Outputs
For the SME-TRD, the compensated viscosity values can be shown on the display. Select the line to display the parameter:
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel allows advanced calculations for thermal compensation of density and viscosity.
The 'Select Measurement to Apply Model' dropdown should be set to 'Concentration'.
The 'Select Model from the list' dropdown should be set to 'API Degrees'.
Selecting a model provides information about its specific equation and required coefficients.
The 'Options' list offers common K0 and K1 values for hydrocarbon groups, with a 'Custom' option for manual input.
For the example shown, the K0 coefficient is 613.97.
For the example shown, the K1 coefficient is 0.0000.
The reference temperature for water density in the example is 60 °F.
The water density at the reference temperature in the example is 999.07 Kg/m3.
The API degrees (APIdg) are calculated using the formula: 141.5 / SG60 - 131.5.
Clicking 'Upload Model' loads the configured model settings into the sensor electronics.
The 'Calculated Value' is refreshed after uploading a model.
Clicking 'Load Configuration' updates the display and channel configurations in the sensor electronics.
The 'Calculation History' button allows opening a file containing previously uploaded models.
For the SME-TRD, compensated viscosity values can be displayed by selecting the appropriate line.
